description:
# Sync & Conflict Resolution System

Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.

## Rationale
Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.

## User Stories
- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages

subtasks.title: Design sync state management architecture
subtasks.description: Define sync states (idle, syncing, success, failed, pending), data types to sync, and state management approach using Redux/Zustand
acceptance_criteria: Data types for sync identified (trips, activities, notes, etc.)
subtasks.notes: Created SyncStatus enum with 5 states (idle, syncing, success, failed, pending). Created SyncState class following AuthState pattern with immutability, copyWith, and factory constructors. Created SyncEntityType enum defining 9 syncable entity types (authTokens, profile, trip, tripPlanning, travelNote, locationUpdate, travelPreference, companions, sharedTrips) with priorities, offline support, and conflict resolution strategies. Used Riverpod for state management (not Redux/Zustand as those are React-specific). Added comprehensive SYNC_ARCHITECTURE.md documentation (12KB). All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Create sync service with operation queue
subtasks.description: Implement SyncService class with queue management for pending operations, FIFO processing, and batch operation support
acceptance_criteria: SyncService class created with queue methods
Operations can be added to queue
subtasks.notes: Implemented SyncService abstract interface (180 lines) and SyncServiceImpl (501 lines) with complete queue management. Features: FIFO processing with priority-based sorting, configurable batch operations (default 50), retry logic (max 5 attempts), pause/resume, real-time status tracking via streams, comprehensive error handling, and SyncQueueConfig with sensible defaults. Includes SyncOperation model (295 lines) with factory constructors and JSON serialization. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Implement operation persistence layer
subtasks.description: Persist operation queue to AsyncStorage/SQLite for recovery across app restarts

subtasks.title: Add network connectivity detection
subtasks.description: Integrate NetInfo or equivalent to detect online/offline status and trigger sync when connection restored

subtasks.title: Implement conflict detection logic
subtasks.description: Detect when same entity was modified on multiple devices using version vectors or last-write-wins timestamps
acceptance_criteria: Conflicts detected when local and remote versions diverged

subtasks.title: Create conflict resolution strategies
subtasks.description: Implement multiple strategies: last-write-wins, manual resolution, automatic merge (for non-overlapping fields)
acceptance_criteria: Strategy selection based on conflict type

subtasks.title: Build conflict resolution UI components
subtasks.description: Create modal/dialog showing both versions of conflicted data with side-by-side comparison and user choice controls

subtasks.title: Implement user decision handling
subtasks.description: Process user's conflict resolution choice and apply to local and remote data
acceptance_criteria: User choice applied to data store

subtasks.title: Implement exponential backoff retry mechanism
subtasks.description: Retry failed sync operations with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s) and max retry limit
acceptance_criteria: Retry logic with exponential backoff implemented

subtasks.title: Add error categorization and messaging
subtasks.description: Categorize errors (network, auth, server, validation) and provide user-friendly messages
acceptance_criteria: User-friendly error messages for each type

subtasks.title: Create actionable error displays
subtasks.description: Build UI components showing sync errors with retry buttons, dismiss options, and help links

subtasks.title: Add manual sync trigger
subtasks.description: Provide pull-to-refresh and sync button in settings for manual sync initiation

subtasks.title: Create sync status indicator components
subtasks.description: Build visual indicators (icons, badges, progress bars) showing sync state

subtasks.title: Add real-time status updates
subtasks.description: Update status indicators immediately on sync state changes using reactive state management
acceptance_criteria: Status changes reflected in UI immediately

subtasks.title: Implement persistent status across app restarts
subtasks.description: Save last sync status and restore on app launch to show if sync was previously failed
acceptance_criteria: Failed status shown on app restart

subtasks.title: Add sync history and logging
subtasks.description: Maintain log of recent sync operations with timestamps for debugging and user transparency

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for sync logic
subtasks.description: Test sync service, conflict detection, resolution strategies, and retry logic
acceptance_criteria: Conflict detection tests with various scenarios

subtasks.title: Create integration tests for conflict scenarios
subtasks.description: Test end-to-end conflict scenarios with mocked backend and multiple clients

subtasks.title: Implement edge case coverage
subtasks.description: Test stale data handling, partial syncs, corrupted queue data, and large batch operations

subtasks.title: Add end-to-end testing with network simulation
subtasks.description: Use React Native TestUtils or Detox to test full sync flow with simulated network conditions
